lines.text: [Scene: Central Perk, everyone but Phoebe is there.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: (entering) Hi guys!

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Hey, Pheebs! Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Hey. Oh, oh, how'd it go?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Um, not so good. He walked me to the subway and said 'We should do this again!'

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Ohh. Ouch.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: What? He said 'we should do it again', that's good, right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Uh, no. Loosely translated 'We should do this again' means 'You will never see me naked'.
lines.korean: 아니. 의역하면 '또 만나자'는 '넌 날 벗은 모습으로 절대 못 볼 거야'라는 뜻이야.
lines.explanation: loosely translated = (직역이 아니라) 의역하면/속뜻을 풀이하면. 데이트에서 하는 빈말의 진짜 속뜻을 농담으로 풀어주는 장면.
examples: Loosely translated, 'I'm busy' means 'I don't want to go'.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Since when?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Since always. It's like dating language. Y'know, like 'It's not you' means 'It is you'.
lines.korean: 원래 늘 그래. 그게 연애 화법이라는 거야. 그 왜, '너 때문이 아니야'가 사실 '너 때문이야'인 것처럼.
lines.explanation: dating language = 연인 사이에서 돌려 말하는 관용적 화법. 'It's not you(네 탓이 아니야)'는 헤어질 때 쓰는 대표적 상투어로, 실은 상대 탓이라는 농담.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Or 'You're such a nice guy' means 'I'm gonna be dating leather-wearing alcoholics and complaining about them to you'.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Or, or, y'know, um, 'I think we should see other people' means 'Ha, ha, I already am'.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: And everybody knows this?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Yeah. Cushions the blow.
lines.korean: 응. 충격을 좀 덜어주는 거지.
lines.explanation: cushion the blow (= soften the blow) = (나쁜 소식의) 충격을 완화하다.
examples: He broke the news gently to cushion the blow.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Yeah, it's like when you're a kid, and your parents put your dog to sleep, and they tell you it went off to live on some farm.
lines.korean: 어릴 때 부모님이 키우던 개를 안락사시키고는, 개가 어디 농장 가서 산다고 둘러대는 거랑 같지.
lines.explanation: put (a pet) to sleep = (동물을) 안락사시키다(완곡 표현). 'went off to live on a farm'은 그 사실을 아이에게 숨기려고 부모가 둘러대는 전형적인 거짓말 클리셰.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: That's funny, that, no, because, uh, our parents actually did, uh, send our dog off to live on a farm.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Uh, Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: What? Wh- hello? The Millners' farm in Connecticut? The Millners, they had this unbelievable farm, they had horses, and, and rabbits that he could chase and it was- it w- .....Oh my God, Chi Chi!

lines.text: Opening Credits
[Scene: Chandler and Joey's, Chandler is helping Joey rehearse for a part.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: "So how does it feel knowing you're about to die?"

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: "Warden, in five minutes my pain will be over. But you'll have to live with the knowledge that you sent an honest man to die."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Hey, that was really good!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Thanks! Let's keep going.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Okay. "So. Whaddya want from me, Damone, huh?"

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: "I just wanna go back to my cell. 'Cause in my cell, I can smoke."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: "Smoke away."
lines.korean: 마음껏 피워.
lines.explanation: 동사 + away = '마음껏/실컷 ~해'. Smoke away = 실컷 피워, Ask away = 얼마든지 물어봐.
examples: You have questions? Ask away.

lines.text: (Joey takes out a pack of cigarettes and a lighter. He fumbles and drops the lighter. Then he lights a cigarett, takes a drag, and coughs.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: I think this is probably why Damone smokes in his cell alone.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Relax your hand!

lines.text: (Joey lets his wrist go limp.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Not so much!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Whoah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Alright, now try taking a puff.

lines.text: (Joey tries and visibly winces.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Alright.. okay. No. Give it to me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: No no no, I am not giving you a cigarette.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: It's fine, it's fine. Look, do you wanna get this part, or not? Here.

lines.text: (Joey reluctantly gives him the cigarette.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Don't think of it as a cigarette. Think of it as the thing that's been missing from your hand. When you're holding it, you feel right. You feel complete.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Y'miss it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Nah, not so much. Alright, now we smoke. (Takes a puff.) Oh.. my.. God. (He continues to smoke.)

lines.text: [Scene, Central Perk, everyone except Phoebe and Rachel is there.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: No, no, no. They say it's the same as the distance from the tip of a guy's thumb to the tip of his index finger.

lines.text: (The guys stretch out their fingers.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: That's ridiculous!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Can I use.. either thumb?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: (carrying a tray of drinks) Alright, don't tell me, don't tell me! (Starts handing them out.) Decaf cappucino for Joey.. Coffee black.. Late.. And an iced tea. I'm getting pretty good at this!

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Yeah. Yeah, excellent.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: (leaving to serve others) Good for me!

lines.text: (The gang swaps all the drinks for what they ordered as Phoebe enters. She sits down without saying hi.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Y'okay, Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Yeah- no- I'm just- it's, I haven't worked- It's my bank.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: What did they do to you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: It's nothing, it's just- Okay. I'm going through my mail, and I open up their monthly, you know, STATEMENT-

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Easy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: - and there's five hundred extra dollars in my account.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh, Satan's minions at work again...
lines.korean: 오, 사탄의 졸개들이 또 일을 벌이는군…
lines.explanation: minion = (우두머리의) 졸개/부하. 은행을 '사탄의 졸개들'이라 부르며 비꼬는 챈들러식 농담.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Yes, 'cause now I have to go down there, and deal with them.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: What are you talking about? Keep it!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: It's not mine, I didn't earn it, if I kept it, it would be like stealing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Yeah, but if you spent it, it would be like shopping!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Okay. Okay, let's say I bought a really great pair of shoes. Do you know what I'd hear, with every step I took? 'Not-mine. Not-mine. Not-mine.' And even if I was happy, okay, and, and skipping- 'Not-not-mine, not-not-mine, not-not-mine, not-not-mine'...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: We're with you. We got it.

lines.text: (Chandler leans over the back of the couch out of sight.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Okay. I'd- just- I'd never be able to enjoy it. It would be like this giant karmic debt.
lines.korean: 그건 엄청난 업보의 빚 같은 게 될 거야.
lines.explanation: karmic debt = 카르마(업)의 빚 — 불교·힌두교의 업보 개념으로, 나쁜 짓을 하면 나중에 갚게 된다는 믿음. 영적인 피비다운 표현.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Chandler, what are you doing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (puling him up) Hey. Whaddya doing?

lines.text: (Chandler tries to shrug nonchalantly but eventually he has to exhale a mouthful of smoke.)

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Oh! Oh, God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: What is this?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: I'm smoking. I'm smoking, I'm smoking.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Oh, I can't believe you! You've been so good, for three years!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: And this- is my reward!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Hold on a second, alright? Just think about what you went through the last time you quit.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Okay, so this time I won't quit!

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Ohhh! Put it out!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: All right! I'm putting it out, I'm putting it out. (He drops it in Phoebe's coffee.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Oh, no! I- I can't drink this now!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Alright. I'm gonna go change, I've got a date.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: This Alan again? How's it goin'?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: 'S'going pretty good, y'know? It's nice, and, we're having fun.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: So when do we get to meet the guy?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Let's see, today's Monday... Never.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Oh, come on! Come on!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: No. Not after what happened with Steve.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: What are you talking about? We love Schhteve! Schhteve was schhexy!.. Sorry.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Look, I don't even know how I feel about him yet. Just give me a chance to figure that out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Well, then can we meet him?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Nope. Schhorry.

lines.text: [Scene: Iridium, Monica and Paula are at work.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: I mean, why should I let them meet him? I mean, I bring a guy home, and within five minutes they're all over him. I mean, they're like- coyotes, picking off the weak members of the herd.
lines.korean: 남자를 집에 데려오면 5분 안에 다들 달려들어. 꼭 코요테가 무리에서 약한 놈을 골라 잡아채는 것 같다니까.
lines.explanation: be all over someone = ~에게 우르르 달려들다/들러붙다. pick off = (하나씩) 골라서 잡아채다/처치하다. the herd = (짐승) 무리. 친구들을 약한 먹잇감을 노리는 코요테에 빗댄 비유.
examples: The reporters were all over the celebrity.

lines.speaker: Paula
lines.english: Listen. As someone who's seen more than her fair share of bad beef, I'll tell you: that is not such a terrible thing. I mean, they're your friends, they're just looking out after you.
lines.korean: 형편없는 남자라면 남들보다 훨씬 많이 겪어본 사람으로서 말하는데, 그건 그렇게 나쁜 게 아니야. 친구들이 널 챙겨주는 거잖아.
lines.explanation: (more than) one's fair share of ~ = (남들보다) 많이/충분히 겪다. bad beef = (소고기에 빗대어) 형편없는 남자. look out for someone = ~를 챙기다/지켜주다 (대사의 'look out after'는 look out for/after가 섞인 구어).
examples: I've had my fair share of bad luck.
Good friends look out for each other.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: I know. I just wish that once, I'd bring a guy home that they actually liked.

lines.speaker: Paula
lines.english: Well, you do realise the odds of that happening are a little slimmer if they never get to meet the guy..
lines.korean: 친구들이 그 남자를 아예 못 만나면, 그렇게 될 가능성은 좀 더 희박해진다는 거 알지.
lines.explanation: the odds (of) = 가능성/확률. slim = (가능성이) 희박한, slimmer = 더 희박한.
examples: The odds of winning are slim.

lines.text: [Scene: Monica and Rachel's, Chandler is smoking out on the balcony, Phoebe is absent.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Let it go, Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Yeah, well, you didn't know Chi Chi.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Do you all promise?

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Yeah! We promise! We'll be good!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (shouts to Chandler) Chandler? Do you promise to be good?

lines.text: (Chandler makes a 'Cross my heart' sign. It starts to rain and he taps on the window.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: You can come in, but your filter-tipped little buddy has to stay outside!

lines.text: (Chandler sulkilty picks up a garbage can lid and uses it as an umbrella.)
(Phoebe enters, walks to the couch, sits down, and begins to read a letter without saying hi.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Hey, Pheebs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: 'Dear Ms. Buffay. Thank you for calling attention to our error. We have credited your account with five hundred dollars. We're sorry for the inconvenience, and hope you'll accept this- (Searches in her purse) -football phone as our free gift.' Do you believe this?! Now I have a thousand dollars, and a football phone!
lines.korean: '계좌에 500달러를 입금해 드렸습니다… 사은품으로 이 미식축구공 전화기를 받아 주세요.' 이게 말이 돼?!
lines.explanation: credit one's account = (은행이) 계좌에 입금 처리하다. football phone = 미식축구공 모양 전화기 — 1990년대 Sports Illustrated 잡지 구독 사은품으로 유명했던 물건.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: What bank is this?

lines.text: (The intercom buzzes.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Hey. It's him. (On the intercom) Who is it?

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.english: (on the intercom) It's Alan.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: (shouting to Chandler) Chandler! He's here!

lines.text: (Chandler comes in, dripping wet.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (to all) Okay, please be good, please. Just remember how much you all like me.

lines.text: (She opens the door and Alan enters.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Hi. Alan, this is everybody. Everybody, this is Alan.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.english: Hi.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Hi, Alan.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.english: I've heard schho much about all you guyschh!
lines.korean: 여러분 얘기 정말 많이 들었어요!
lines.explanation: Alan의 대사에 섞인 'schh' 철자는 혀가 짧은 듯한(슬러시) 발음을 글자로 표기한 것. 공교롭게 앞서 챈들러가 흉내 낸 말투(Schhteve)와 겹치는 설정.

lines.text: (Everyone laughs.)
[Time lapse, Alan is leaving.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (to Alan) Thanks. I'll call you tomorrow. (Alan exits, to all) Okay. Okay, let's let the Alan-bashing begin. Who's gonna take the first shot, hmm?
lines.korean: 자, 이제 앨런 험담 시작하자. 누가 먼저 포문 열래?
lines.explanation: bashing = 헐뜯기/맹비난 ('Alan-bashing' = 앨런 험담). take the first shot = 먼저 한마디 하다/포문을 열다.
examples: The press loves a good celebrity-bashing.

lines.text: (Silence.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: C'mon!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: ...I'll go. Let's start with the way he kept picking at- no, I'm sorry, I can't do this, can't do this. We loved him.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Loved him! Yeah! He's great!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Wait a minute! We're talking about someone that I'm going out with?

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: And did you notice...? (She spreads her thumb and index finger.)

lines.speaker: The Guys
lines.english: (reluctantly) Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Know what was great? The way his smile was kinda crooked.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Yes, yes! Like the man in the shoe!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: ...What shoe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: From the nursery rhyme. 'There was a crooked man, Who had a crooked smile, Who lived in a shoe, For a... while...'
lines.korean: 그 동요에 나오잖아. '비뚤어진 남자가 있었는데…' 하는.
lines.explanation: nursery rhyme = 전래 동요/자장가. 피비가 'There Was a Crooked Man'이라는 영미권 전통 동요를 (엉터리로) 인용하는 장면. (crooked = 비뚤어진)

lines.text: (Dubious pause.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: ...So I think Alan will become the yardstick against which all future boyfriends will be measured.
lines.korean: 앨런이 앞으로 만날 남자친구들을 재는 잣대가 될 것 같아.
lines.explanation: yardstick = (평가의) 잣대/기준. 'the yardstick against which X is measured' = X를 가늠하는 기준.
examples: Profit isn't the only yardstick of success.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: What future boyfriends? Nono, I th- I think this could be, y'know, it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Really!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh, yeah. I'd marry him just for his David Hasselhof impression alone. You know I'm gonna be doing that at parties, right? (Does the impression)
lines.korean: 걔 데이비드 해셀호프 성대모사 하나만으로도 결혼하겠어.
lines.explanation: David Hasselhoff = 미국 배우(드라마 'Knight Rider', 'Baywatch'로 유명). impression = 성대모사/흉내.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: You know what I like most about him, though?

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: The way he makes me feel about myself.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Yeah...

lines.text: Commercial Break
[Scene: Central Perk, Monica is alone as Ross, Rachel, Chandler, and Joey enter dejectedly in softball gear.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Hi.. how was the game?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Well..

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: WE WON!! Thank you! Yes!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Fantastic! I have one question: How is that possible?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Alan.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: He was unbelievable. He was like that-that-that Bugs Bunny cartoon where Bugs is playing all the positions, right, but instead of Bugs it was first base-Alan, second base-Alan, third base-...
lines.korean: 벅스 버니가 야구에서 모든 포지션을 혼자 다 보는 그 만화 있잖아. 근데 벅스 대신 1루도 앨런, 2루도 앨런, 3루도…
lines.explanation: Bugs Bunny = 미국 애니메이션 캐릭터. 벅스가 야구 수비를 혼자 다 하는 'Baseball Bugs' 편을 가리킴 — 앨런이 혼자 다 잘했다는 뜻.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: I mean, it-it was like, it was like he made us into a team.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Yep, we sure showed those Hassidic jewellers a thing or two about softball..
lines.korean: 그 하시딕 보석상들한테 소프트볼이 뭔지 제대로 본때를 보여줬지.
lines.explanation: show someone a thing or two = ~에게 본때를 보여주다/한 수 가르쳐 주다. Hassidic jewellers = 하시딕(정통파) 유대인 보석상 — 뉴욕 보석 거리에 많아 동네 리그 상대팀을 가리킴.
examples: She showed them a thing or two about negotiating.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Can I ask you guys a question? D'you ever think that Alan is maybe.. sometimes..

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: ..I dunno, a little too Alan?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Well, no. That's impossible. You can never be too Alan.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Yeah, it's his, uh, innate Alan-ness that-that-that we adore.
lines.korean: 맞아, 우리가 좋아하는 건 바로 걔 특유의 '앨런다움'이지.
lines.explanation: innate = 타고난. '-ness'는 '~함/~인 성질'을 만드는 접미사인데, 여기선 이름 Alan에 붙여 'Alan-ness(앨런다움)'라는 우스운 조어를 만든 말장난.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: I personally could have a gallon of Alan.

lines.text: [Scene: A street, Phoebe walks up to a homeless person (Lizzie) she knows.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Hey, Lizzie.

lines.speaker: Lizzie
lines.english: Hey, Weird Girl.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: I brought you alphabet soup.

lines.speaker: Lizzie
lines.english: Did you pick out the vowels?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Yes. But I left in the Ys. 'Cause, y'know, "sometimes y". Uh, I also have something else for you. (She searches in her purse.)
lines.korean: 근데 Y는 남겨뒀어. 왜, '가끔은 y'라고 하잖아.
lines.explanation: 영어 모음을 외울 때 'a, e, i, o, u, and sometimes y(가끔은 y)'라고 가르치는데, 피비가 알파벳 수프에서 모음을 골라내며 그 규칙을 농담으로 적용한 것.

lines.speaker: Lizzie
lines.english: Saltines?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: No, but would you like a thousand dollars and a football phone?

lines.speaker: Lizzie
lines.english: What? (She opens the envelope Phoebe has given her.) Oh my God, there's really money in here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: I know.

lines.speaker: Lizzie
lines.english: Weird Girl, what are you doing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: No, I want you to have it. I don't want it.

lines.speaker: Lizzie
lines.english: No, no, I ha-I have to give you something.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Oh, that's fine, no.

lines.speaker: Lizzie
lines.english: Would you like my tin-foil hat?
lines.korean: 내 은박지 모자 줄까?
lines.explanation: tin-foil hat = 은박지(쿠킹포일)로 만든 모자 — '전파·감시로부터 머리를 지킨다'고 믿는 편집증·음모론자의 상징. 리지가 길에서 지내는 괴짜임을 보여줌.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: No. 'Cause you need that. No, it's okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Lizzie
lines.english: Please, let me do something.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Okay, alright, you buy me a soda, and then we're even. Okay?
lines.korean: 그럼 네가 음료수 하나 사줘, 그럼 우리 비긴 거야.
lines.explanation: we're even = (주고받은 게 같아) 비겼다/빚진 거 없다.
examples: Pay me back the five bucks and we're even.

lines.speaker: Lizzie
lines.english: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Okay.

lines.text: [Scene: Chandler's office, Chandler looks around, opens his desk drawer, takes a puff of a cigarette, sprays around some air freshener, and takes some breath spray. He types for a little while, opens the drawer again, and takes another drag of the cigarette. While not paying attention, he sprays the breath spray around the room, takes a squirt of air freshener and gags.]
[Scene: A Street, Phoebe and Lizzie are at a hot dog vendor.]

lines.speaker: Lizzie
lines.english: Keep the change. (To Phoebe) Sure you don't wanna pretzel?
lines.korean: 잔돈은 가지세요.
lines.explanation: Keep the change. = (거스름돈을 안 받을 때) 잔돈은 가지세요 — 식당·택시 등에서 팁처럼 흔히 쓰는 표현.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: No, I'm fine.

lines.speaker: Lizzie
lines.english: (leaves) See ya.

lines.text: (Phoebe opens the can and reacts.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Huh!

lines.text: [Scene: Central Perk, Phoebe is telling everyone about her discovery.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: A thumb?!

lines.text: (Phoebe nods.)

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Eww!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: I know! I know, I opened it up and there it was, just floating in there, like this tiny little hitch-hiker!
lines.korean: 안에 그냥 둥둥 떠 있더라고, 꼭 조그만 히치하이커처럼!
lines.explanation: hitch-hiker = 차를 얻어 타려고 엄지를 치켜드는 사람. 캔에서 나온 '엄지손가락(thumb)'을 엄지를 드는 히치하이킹 동작에 빗댄 말장난.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Well, maybe it's a contest, y'know? Like, collect all five?
lines.korean: 뭐 일종의 이벤트 아닐까? '다섯 개 모으기' 같은?
lines.explanation: 'Collect all five/six!'는 과자·완구 등에서 여러 종을 다 모으게 유도하는 마케팅 문구. 손가락이 또 나올지도 모른다는 챈들러의 농담.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Does, um, anyone wanna see?

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Nooo!

lines.text: (Chandler lights a cigarette.)

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Oh, hey, don't do that! Cut it out!
lines.korean: 야, 그러지 마! 그만해!
lines.explanation: Cut it out! = 그만해!/집어치워! (짜증 섞인 제지).
examples: Cut it out, you two!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: It's worse than the thumb!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Hey, this is so unfair!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Oh, why is it unfair?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: So I have a flaw! Big deal! Like Joey's constant knuckle-cracking isn't annoying? And Ross, with his over-pronouncing every single word? And Monica, with that snort when she laughs? I mean, what the hell is that thing? ...I accept all those flaws, why can't you accept me for this?
lines.korean: 그래, 나도 단점 하나 있다! 그게 뭐 대수라고! …모니카는 웃을 때 그 콧소리 내잖아?
lines.explanation: Big deal! = 그게 뭐 대수라고/별것 아니네 (반어적 무시). snort = 웃을 때 코로 '킁' 하고 내는 소리. flaw = 결점/흠.
examples: So you're late. Big deal.

lines.text: (An awkward silence ensues.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: ...Does the knuckle-cracking bother everybody?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Well, I-I could live without it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Well, is it, like, a little annoying, or is it like when Phoebe chews her hair?

lines.text: (Phoebe spits out her hair.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Oh, now, don't listen to him, Pheebs, I think it's endearing.
lines.korean: 신경 쓰지 마, 피비. 난 그게 오히려 사랑스럽다고 생각해.
lines.explanation: endearing = (행동·버릇이) 사랑스러운/정이 가는.
examples: Her clumsiness is oddly endearing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Oh, (Imitating Ross) "you do, do you"?

lines.text: (Monica laughs and snorts.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: You know, there's nothing wrong with speaking correctly.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: "Indeed there isn't"... I should really get back to work.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Yeah, 'cause otherwise someone might get what they actually ordered.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Ohh-ho-hooohhh. The hair comes out, and the gloves come on.
lines.korean: 머리카락이 나오고, 이제 본격적으로 싸울 태세네.
lines.explanation: the gloves are/come off = (봐주기 없이) 본격적으로 맞붙다 (권투 글러브를 벗고 맨주먹으로 싸운다는 데서 유래). 피비가 화나면 머리를 씹는 설정과 엮어 'gloves come on'으로 비틂.
examples: Once the talks broke down, the gloves came off.

lines.text: (They degenerate into bickering and Chandler happily starts to smoke, undisturbed.)
[Scene: Iridium, Monica and Paula are working.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Did you ever go out with a guy your friends all really like?

lines.speaker: Paula
lines.english: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Okay.. Well, I'm going out with a guy my friends all really like.

lines.speaker: Paula
lines.english: Waitwait.. we talking about the coyotes here? All right, a cow got through!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Can you believe it? ...Y'know what? I just don't feel the thing. I mean, they feel the thing, I don't feel the thing.

lines.speaker: Paula
lines.english: Honey.. you should always feel the thing. Listen, if that's how you feel about the guy, Monica, dump him!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: I know.. it's gonna be really hard.

lines.speaker: Paula
lines.english: Well, he's a big boy, he'll get over it.
lines.korean: 걘 다 큰 어른이니까, 알아서 잘 추스를 거야.
lines.explanation: a big boy/girl = 다 큰 어른(스스로 감당할 수 있는). get over it = (실연·충격을) 극복하다/잊다.
examples: Don't worry about him - he'll get over it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: No, he'll be fine. It's the other five I'm worried about.

lines.text: [Scene: Cental Perk, Joey and Ross are persecuting Chandler about his smoking.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Do you have any respect for your body?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Don't you realise what you're-you're doing to yourself?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Hey, y'know, I have had it with you guys and your cancer and your emphysema and your heart disease. The bottom line is, smoking is cool, and you know it.
lines.korean: 야, 너희들이랑 암이니 폐기종이니 심장병이니 하는 얘기 이제 지긋지긋해. 결론은, 담배는 멋있고 너희도 그걸 알잖아.
lines.explanation: have had it with ~ = ~에 질리다/넌더리 나다. the bottom line is ~ = 결론은/핵심은 ~다.
examples: I've had it with these delays.
The bottom line is, we can't afford it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: (holding the phone out to Chandler) Chandler? It's Alan, he wants to speak to you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Really? He does? (taking the phone) Hey, buddy, what's up! Oh, she told you about that, huh. Well, yeah, I have one now and then. Well, yeah, now. Well, it's not that big- ..well, that's true,.. Gee, y'know, no-one- no-one's ever put it like that before. Well, okay, thanks! (He hands the phone back and stubs out his cigarette.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: (to Ross, who has wandered up) God, he's good.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: If only he were a woman.
lines.korean: 걔가 여자이기만 했어도 (좋았을 텐데).
lines.explanation: If only + 과거형(were) = ~이기만 하면/했더라면 좋을 텐데 (현재 사실의 반대를 아쉬워하는 가정법). 가정법에선 was 대신 were를 흔히 씀.
examples: If only I were taller.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Yeah.

lines.text: (They give each other a dubious look.)
[Scene: Monica and Rachel's, everyond except Monica and Joey is watching Lambchop.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Ooh, Lambchop. How old is that sock? If I had a sock on my hand for thirty years it'd be talking too.
lines.korean: 오, 램찹이네. 저 양말 도대체 몇 년 된 거야?
lines.explanation: Lamb Chop = 미국 어린이 프로그램의 양 모양 양말 인형(진행자 Shari Lewis). 손인형이라 '양말'이라고 농담한 것.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Okay. I think it's time to change somebody's nicotine patch. (Does so.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (entering) Hey. Where's Joey?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Joey ate my last stick of gum, so I killed him. Do you think that was wrong?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: I think he's across the hall.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Thanks. (Goes to fetch him.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: (finishing changing Chandler's nicotine patch) There y'go.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: (deadpan) Ooh, I'm alive with pleasure now.
lines.korean: (무표정하게) 오, 이제 기쁨이 넘쳐 살아나는군.
lines.explanation: deadpan = 무표정하게 (농담을) 던지는. 'Alive with pleasure!'는 옛 담배(Newport) 광고 슬로건으로, 니코틴 패치를 붙인 챈들러가 이를 비꼬아 읊은 것.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Hey Pheebs, you gonna have the rest of that Pop-Tart?.. Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Does anyone want the rest of this Pop-Tart?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Hey, I might!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Sorry. ..Y'know, those stupid soda people gave me seven thousand dollars for the thumb.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: You're kidding. Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: And on my way over here, I stepped in gum. ...What is up with the universe?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: (dragged in by Monica, he has just gotten out of the shower) What's going on?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Nothing. I just think it's nice when we're all here together.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Even nicer when everyone gets to wear their underwear..

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Uh, Joey..

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Oh, God! (Hurriedly closes his legs.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (turns off the TV) Okay..

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Oh! That was Lambchop!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Please, guys, we have to talk.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Wait, wait, I'm getting a deja vu...no, I'm not.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Alright, we have to talk.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: There it is!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Okay. It's-it's about Alan. There's something that you should know. I mean, there's really no easy way to say this.. uh.. I've decided to break up with Alan.

lines.text: (They all gasp and clutch each other.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Is there somebody else?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: No, nononono.. it's just.. things change. People change.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: We didn't change..

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: So that's it? It's over? Just like that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: You know.. you let your guard down, you start to really care about someone, and I just- I- (starts chewing her hair)
lines.korean: 경계를 풀고 누군가를 진심으로 아끼기 시작하면…
lines.explanation: let your guard down = 경계를 풀다/마음의 빗장을 내리다.
examples: He finally let his guard down and opened up.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Look, I- I could go on pretending-

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Okay!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: -but that wouldn't be fair to me, it wouldn't be fair to Alan- It wouldn't be fair to you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Who-who wants fair? Y'know, I just want things back. Y'know, the way they were.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: I'm sorry..

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: (sarcastic) Oh, she's sorry! I feel better!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: (tearful) I just can't believe this! I mean, with the holidays coming up- I wanted him to meet my family-

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: I'll meet somone else. There'll be other Alans.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Oh, yeah! Right!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Are you guys gonna be okay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Hey hey, we'll be fine. We're just gonna need a little time.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (dubious) I understand.

lines.text: [Scene: A Restaurant, Monica is breaking the news to Alan.]

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.english: Wow.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: I'm, I'm really sorry.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.english: Yeah, I'm sorry too. But, I gotta tell you, I am a little relieved.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Relieved?

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.english: Yeah, well, I had a great time with you.. I just can't stand your friends.
lines.korean: 너랑은 정말 즐거웠어… 그런데 네 친구들은 도저히 못 견디겠더라.
lines.explanation: can't stand ~ = ~을 못 견디다/질색이다. 앨런이 모니카는 좋아했지만 친구들은 질색이었다는 반전.
examples: I can't stand the smell of cigarettes.

lines.text: Closing Credits
[Scene: Monica and Rachel's, everyone is mopping around and eating ice cream.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Remember when we went to Central Park and rented boats?.. That was fun.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Yeah. He could row like a viking.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (entering) Hi.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Mmm.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: So how'd it go?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Oh, y'know..

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Did he mention us?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: He said he's really gonna miss you guys. (dubious look)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: You had a rough day, huh.. c'mere. (She sits down and Ross strokes her forehead.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: ...That's it. I'm getting cigarettes.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: No no no!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: (leaving) I don't care, I don't care! Game's over! I'm weak! I've gotta smoke! I've gotta have the smoke!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: (shouting as he leaves) If you never smoke again I'll give you seven thousand dollars!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: (returns) Yeah, alright.

lines.text: End
